{"step_by_step_thinking": "Based on the information provided in the documents, the dorsal interossei muscles are located in the middle three digits of the hand. Specifically, Document [14] (Title: Anatomy, Shoulder and Upper Limb, Hand Dorsal Interossei Muscle -- Review Questions) states 'Several clinical examinations are used to assess ulnar nerve function. Clinicians can evaluate the dorsal interossei by instructing patients to abduct their 2nd through 5th digits against resistance.' This indicates that the dorsal interossei are present in the 2nd through 5th digits, which correspond to the index, middle, ring, and little fingers. Therefore, the middle finger has two dorsal interossei.", "answer_choice": "C"}
